heading: Transition to sustainable fisheries
label: 1861a Transition to sustainable fisheries

children.heading: Allowable cause
children.label: A Allowable cause
children.text: The term “allowable cause” means a natural cause, discrete anthropogenic cause, or undetermined cause, including a cause that occurred not more than 5 years prior to the date of a request for a fishery resource disaster determination that affected such applicable fishery.

children.label: i
children.text: that could not have been addressed or prevented by fishery management measures; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that is otherwise beyond the control of fishery managers to mitigate through conservation and management measures, including regulatory restrictions imposed as a result of judicial action or to protect human health or marine animals, plants, or habitats.

children.label: i
children.text: is an unexpected large decrease in fish stock biomass or other change that results in significant loss of access to the fishery resource, which may include loss of fishing vessels and gear for a substantial period of time and results in significant revenue loss or negative subsistence impact due to an allowable cause; and

children.label: I
children.text: reasonably predictable, foreseeable, and recurrent fishery cyclical variations in species distribution or stock abundance; or

children.label: II
children.text: reductions in fishing opportunities resulting from conservation and management measures taken pursuant to this chapter.

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: D Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given such term in , and the term “Tribal” means of or pertaining to such an Indian tribe.section 5130 of title 25

children.label: I
children.text: a hurricane;

children.label: II
children.text: a flood;

children.label: III
children.text: a harmful algal bloom;

children.label: IV
children.text: a tsunami;

children.label: V
children.text: a hypoxic zone;

children.label: VI
children.text: a drought;

children.label: VII
children.text: El Niño effects on water temperature;

children.label: VIII
children.text: a marine heat wave; or

children.label: IX
children.text: disease; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not mean a normal or cyclical variation in a species distribution or stock abundance.

children.heading: 12-month revenue loss
children.label: F 12-month revenue loss
children.text: The term “12-month revenue loss” means the percentage reduction, as applicable, in commercial, charter, headboat, or processor revenue for the affected fishery for the 12 months during which the fishery resource disaster occurred, when compared to average annual revenue in the most recent 5 years when no fishery resource disaster occurred or equivalent for stocks with cyclical life histories.

children.heading: Undetermined cause
children.label: G Undetermined cause
children.text: The term “undetermined cause” means a cause in which the current state of knowledge does not allow the Secretary to identify the exact cause, and there is no current conclusive evidence supporting a possible cause of the fishery resource disaster.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall have the authority to determine the existence, extent, and beginning and end dates of a fishery resource disaster under this subsection in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: B Availability of funds
children.text: After the Secretary determines that a fishery resource disaster has occurred, the Secretary is authorized to make sums available, from funds appropriated for such purposes, to be used by the affected State, Indian Tribe, or interstate marine fisheries commission, or by the Secretary in cooperation with the affected State, Indian Tribe, or interstate marine fisheries commission.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: C Savings clause
children.text: The requirements under this paragraph and paragraphs (3), (4), and (5) shall take effect only with respect to fishery resource disaster determination requests submitted after .December 29, 2022

children.heading: Initiation of a fishery resource disaster review
children.label: 3 Initiation of a fishery resource disaster review
I
children.text: the Governor of an affected State;

children.label: II
children.text: an official resolution of an Indian Tribe; or

children.label: III
children.text: any other comparable elected or politically appointed representative as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: 1 year after the date of the conclusion of the fishing season;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a distinct cause that occurs during more than 1 consecutive fishing season, 2 years after the date of the conclusion of the fishing season for which the request for a fishery resource disaster determination is made; or

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a complete fishery closure, 1 year after the date on which that closure is determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: identification of all presumed affected fish stocks;

children.label: ii
children.text: identification of the fishery as Federal, non-Federal, or both;

children.label: iii
children.text: the geographical boundaries of the fishery, as determined by the eligible requester, including geographic boundaries that are smaller than the area represented by the eligible requester;

children.label: iv
children.text: preliminary information on causes of the fishery resource disaster, if known; and

children.label: I
children.text: information demonstrating the occurrence of an unexpected large decrease in fish stock biomass or other change that results in significant loss of access to the fishery resource, which could include the loss of fishing vessels and gear, for a substantial period of time;

children.label: aa
children.text: 12-month revenue loss for the affected fishery; or

children.label: bb
children.text: negative subsistence impact for the affected fishery, or if a fishery resource disaster has occurred at any time in the previous 5-year period, the most recent 5 years when no fishery resource disaster occurred;

children.label: III
children.text: if applicable, information on lost resource tax revenues assessed by local communities, such as a raw fish tax and local sourcing requirements; and

children.label: IV
children.text: if applicable and available, information on affected fishery 12-month revenue loss for charter, headboat, or processors related to the information provided under subclause (I), subject to .section 1881a(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the assistance is so requested;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary is in possession of the required information described in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the data is not available to the requester, in carrying out the complete request under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Initiation of review
children.label: D Initiation of review
children.text: The Secretary shall have the discretion to initiate a fishery resource disaster review without a request.

children.label: i
children.text: acknowledges receipt of the request;

children.label: ii
children.text: provides a regional contact within the National Oceanographic and Atmospheric Administration;

children.label: iii
children.text: outlines the process and timeline by which a request shall be considered; and

children.label: iv
children.text: requests additional information concerning the fishery resource disaster, if the original request is considered incomplete.

children.label: aa
children.text: fishery characteristics;

children.label: bb
children.text: stock assessments;

children.label: cc
children.text: the most recent fishery independent surveys and other fishery resource assessments and surveys conducted by Federal, State, or Tribal officials;

children.label: dd
children.text: estimates of mortality; and

children.label: ee
children.text: overall effects; and

children.label: aa
children.text: landings data;

children.label: bb
children.text: revenue;

children.label: cc
children.text: the number of participants involved;

children.label: AA
children.text: fishers;

children.label: BB
children.text: charter fishing operators;

children.label: CC
children.text: subsistence users;

children.label: DD
children.text: United States fish processors; and

children.label: EE
children.text: an owner of a related fishery infrastructure or business affected by the disaster, such as a marina operator, recreational fishing equipment retailer, or charter, headboat, or tender vessel owner, operator, or crew;

children.label: ee
children.text: an impacted Indian Tribe;

children.label: ff
children.text: other forms of disaster assistance made available to the fishery, including prior awards of disaster assistance for the same event;

children.label: gg
children.text: the length of time the resource, or access to the resource, has been restricted;

children.label: hh
children.text: status of recovery from previous fishery resource disasters;

children.label: ii
children.text: lost resource tax revenues assessed by local communities, such as a raw fish tax; and

children.label: jj
children.text: other appropriate indicators to an affected fishery, as determined by the National Marine Fisheries Service.

children.label: I
children.text: has concluded or there is no defined fishing season applicable to the fishery, not later than 120 days after the Secretary receives a complete request for a fishery resource disaster determination;

children.label: II
children.text: has not concluded, not later than 120 days after the conclusion of the fishing season; or

children.label: III
children.text: is expected to be closed for the entire fishing season, not later than 120 days after the Secretary receives a complete request for a fishery resource disaster determination.

children.heading: Fishery resource disaster determination
children.label: C Fishery resource disaster determination
children.text: The Secretary shall make the determination of a fishery resource disaster based on the criteria for determinations listed in paragraph (5).

children.heading: Notification
children.label: D Notification
children.text: Not later than 14 days after the conclusion of the review under this paragraph, the Secretary shall notify the requester and the Governor of the affected State or Indian Tribe representative of the determination of the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: a natural cause;

children.label: ii
children.text: an anthropogenic cause;

children.label: iii
children.text: a combination of a natural cause and an anthropogenic cause; or

children.label: iv
children.text: an undetermined cause.

children.label: I
children.text: Losses greater than 80 percent may result in a positive determination that a fishery resource disaster has occurred, based on the information provided or analyzed under paragraph (4)(B).

children.label: II
children.text: Losses between 35 percent and 80 percent shall be evaluated to determine whether economic impacts are severe enough to determine that a fishery resource disaster has occurred.

children.label: III
children.text: Losses less than 35 percent shall not be eligible for a determination that a fishery resource disaster has occurred.

children.heading: Charter fishing
children.label: ii Charter fishing
children.text: In making a determination of whether a fishery resource disaster has occurred, the Secretary shall consider the economic impacts to the charter fishing industry to ensure financial coverage for charter fishing businesses.

children.heading: Negative subsistence impacts
children.label: iii Negative subsistence impacts
children.text: In considering negative subsistence impacts, the Secretary shall evaluate the severity of negative impacts to the fishing community instead of applying the revenue loss thresholds described in clause (i).

children.heading: Ineligible fisheries
children.label: C Ineligible fisheries
children.text: A fishery subject to overfishing in any of the 3 years preceding the date of a determination under this subsection is not eligible for a determination of whether a fishery resource disaster has occurred unless the Secretary determines that overfishing was not a contributing factor to the fishery resource disaster.

children.heading: Exceptional circumstances
children.label: D Exceptional circumstances
children.text: In an exceptional circumstance where substantial economic impacts to the affected fishery and fishing community have been subject to a disaster declaration under another statutory authority, such as in the case of a natural disaster or from the direct consequences of a Federal action taken to prevent, or in response to, a natural disaster for purposes of protecting life and safety, the Secretary may determine a fishery resource disaster has occurred without a request, notwithstanding the requirements under subparagraph (B) and paragraph (3).

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: A Authorization
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate funds available under paragraph (9) for fishery resource disasters.

children.heading: Allocation of appropriated fishery resource disaster assistance
children.label: B Allocation of appropriated fishery resource disaster assistance
I
children.text: the public; and

children.label: II
children.text: representatives of affected fishing communities with a positive disaster determination that is unfunded;

children.heading: Extension of deadline
children.label: ii Extension of deadline
children.text: The Secretary may extend the deadline under clause (i) by 90 days to evaluate and make determinations on eligible requests.

children.label: i
children.text: Direct economic impacts.

children.label: ii
children.text: Uninsured losses.

children.label: iii
children.text: Losses of recreational fishing opportunity.

children.label: iv
children.text: Aquaculture operations revenue loss.

children.label: v
children.text: Direct revenue losses to a fishing community.

children.label: vi
children.text: Treaty obligations.

children.label: vii
children.text: Other economic impacts.

children.label: I
children.text: Objectives and outcomes, with an emphasis on addressing the factors contributing to the fishery resource disaster and minimizing future uninsured losses, if applicable.

children.label: II
children.text: Statement of work.

children.label: III
children.text: Budget details.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall review a spend plan submitted under clause (i) to determine if it is complete and provide notice within 10 days.

children.heading: Incomplete spend plan
children.label: II Incomplete spend plan
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a spend plan submitted under clause (i) is not complete, when providing the notice required by subclause (I), the Secretary shall provide a detailed description of the information that is necessary for the spend plan to be determined complete.

children.heading: Regional contact
children.label: E Regional contact
children.text: If so requested, the Secretary shall provide a regional contact within the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to facilitate review of spend plans and disbursal of funds.

children.heading: Timeline
children.label: I Timeline
children.text: Funds shall be made available to grantees not later than 90 days after the date the Secretary receives a complete spend plan.

children.heading: Review of completed spend plan and apportionment of funds
children.label: II Review of completed spend plan and apportionment of funds
children.text: If so requested, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget may review a completed spend plan concurrently with the Secretary. The Director of the Office of Management and Budget may not delay the timeline described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Method
children.label: ii Method
children.text: The Secretary may provide an allocation of funds under this subsection in the form of a grant, direct payment, cooperative agreement, loan, or contract.

children.label: aa
children.text: Habitat conservation and restoration and other activities, including scientific research, that reduce adverse impacts to the fishery or improve understanding of the affected species or its ecosystem.

children.label: bb
children.text: The collection of fishery information and other activities that improve management of the affected fishery.

children.label: cc
children.text: In a commercial fishery, capacity reduction and other activities that improve management of fishing effort, including funds to offset budgetary costs to refinance a Federal fishing capacity reduction loan or to repay the principal of a Federal fishing capacity reduction loan.

children.label: dd
children.text: Developing, repairing, or improving fishery-related public infrastructure.

children.label: ee
children.text: Direct assistance to a person, fishing community (including assistance for lost fisheries resource levies), or a business to alleviate economic loss incurred as a direct result of a fishery resource disaster, particularly when affected by a circumstance described in paragraph (5)(D) or by negative impacts to subsistence or Indian Tribe ceremonial fishing opportunity.

children.label: ff
children.text: Hatcheries and stock enhancement to help rebuild the affected stock or offset fishing pressure on the affected stock.

children.heading: Displaced fishery employees
children.label: II Displaced fishery employees
children.text: Where appropriate, individuals carrying out the activities described in items (aa) through (dd) of subclause (I) shall be individuals who are, or were, employed in a commercial, charter, or Indian Tribe fishery for which the Secretary has determined that a fishery resource disaster has occurred.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clauses (ii) and (iii), the Federal share of the cost of any activity carried out under the authority of this subsection shall not exceed 75 percent of the cost of that activity.

children.label: I
children.text: no reasonable means are available through which the recipient of the Federal share can meet the non-Federal share requirement; and

children.label: II
children.text: the probable benefit of 100 percent Federal financing outweighs the public interest in imposition of the non-Federal share requirement.

children.label: I
children.text: direct assistance as described in paragraph (6)(F)(iii)(I)(ee); or

children.label: II
children.text: assistance to subsistence or Tribal fisheries.

children.heading: Federal
children.label: i Federal
children.text: Not more than 3 percent of the funds available under this subsection may be used for administrative expenses by the National Oceanographic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: State governments or Indian Tribes
children.label: ii State governments or Indian Tribes
children.text: Of the funds remaining after the use described in clause (i), not more than 5 percent may be used by States, Indian Tribes, or interstate marine fisheries commissions for administrative expenses.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No funds available under this subsection may be used as part of a fishing capacity reduction program in a fishery unless the Secretary determines that adequate conservation and management measures are in place in such fishery.

children.label: I
children.text: prohibit the vessel from being used for fishing in Federal, State, or international waters; and

children.label: aa
children.text: scrapped or otherwise disposed of in a manner approved by the Secretary;

children.label: bb
children.text: donated to a nonprofit organization and thereafter used only for purposes of research, education, or training; or

children.label: cc
children.text: used for another non-fishing purpose provided the Secretary determines that adequate measures are in place to ensure that the vessel cannot reenter any fishery anywhere in the world.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A vessel that is prohibited from fishing under subparagraph (C)(ii)(I) shall not be eligible for a fishery endorsement under .section 12113(a) of title 46

children.heading: Noneffective
children.label: ii Noneffective
children.text: A fishery endorsement for a vessel described in clause (i) shall not be effective.

children.heading: No sale
children.label: iii No sale
children.text: A vessel described in clause (i) shall not be sold to a foreign owner or reflagged.

children.heading: Public information on data collection
children.label: 8 Public information on data collection
children.text: The Secretary shall make available and update as appropriate, information on data collection and submittal best practices for the information described in paragraph (4)(B).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 9 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $377,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.label: A
children.text: is necessary to prevent or end overfishing, rebuild stocks of fish, or achieve measurable and significant improvements in the conservation and management of the fishery;

children.label: i
children.text: will prevent the replacement of fishing capacity removed by the program through a moratorium on new entrants, practicable restrictions on vessel upgrades, and other effort control measures, taking into account the full potential fishing capacity of the fleet; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establishes a specified or target total allowable catch or other measures that trigger closure of the fishery or adjustments to reduce catch; and

children.label: C
children.text: is cost-effective and, in the instance of a program involving an industry fee system, prospectively capable of repaying any debt obligation incurred under .section 53735 of title 46

children.label: A
children.text: the owner of a fishing vessel, if the permit authorizing the participation of the vessel in the fishery is surrendered for permanent revocation and the vessel owner and permit holder relinquish any claim associated with the vessel or permit that could qualify such owner or holder for any present or future limited access system permit in the fishery for which the program is established or in any other fishery and such vessel is (i) scrapped, or (ii) through the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating, subjected to title restrictions (including loss of the vessel’s fisheries endorsement) that permanently prohibit and effectively prevent its use in fishing in federal or state waters, or fishing on the high seas or in the waters of a foreign nation; or11So in original. Probably should be capitalized.1

children.label: B
children.text: the holder of a permit authorizing participation in the fishery, if such permit is surrendered for permanent revocation, and such holder relinquishes any claim associated with the permit and vessel used to harvest fishery resources under the permit that could qualify such holder for any present or future limited access system permit in the fishery for which the program was established.
Participation in the program shall be voluntary, but the Secretary shall ensure compliance by all who do participate.

The harvester proponents of each program and the Secretary shall consult, as appropriate and practicable, with Councils, Federal agencies, State and regional authorities, affected fishing communities, participants in the fishery, conservation organizations, and other interested parties throughout the development and implementation of any program under this section.

children.heading: .—Payment condition
children.label: 5 .—Payment condition
children.text: The Secretary may not make a payment under paragraph (2) with respect to a vessel that will not be scrapped unless the Secretary certifies that the vessel will not be used for fishing in the waters of a foreign nation or fishing on the high seas.

children.label: ii

children.label: iii

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: including measures to prevent the capacity that is being removed from the fishery from moving to other fisheries in the United States, in the waters of a foreign nation, or on the high seas.

children.text: identify, to the extent practicable, and notify all permit or vessel owners who would be affected by the program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make available to such owners information about the industry fee system describing the schedule, procedures, and eligibility requirements for the referendum, the proposed program, and the amount and duration and any other terms and conditions of the proposed fee system.

children.label: B
children.text: The industry fee system shall be considered approved if the referendum votes which are cast in favor of the proposed system constitute at least a majority of the permit holders in the fishery, or 50 percent of the permitted allocation of the fishery, who participated in the fishery.

children.heading: Framework regulations
children.label: 1 Framework regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall propose and adopt framework regulations applicable to the implementation of all programs under this section.

children.heading: Program regulations
children.label: 2 Program regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall implement each program under this section by promulgating regulations that, together with the framework regulations, establish each program and control its implementation.

children.label: i
children.text: the requirements of this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirements of the framework regulations;

children.label: iii
children.text: the characteristics of the fishery and affected fishing communities;

children.label: iv
children.text: the requirements of the applicable fishery management plan and any amendment that such plan may require to support the proposed program;

children.label: v
children.text: the general needs and desires of harvesters in the fishery;

children.label: vi
children.text: the need to minimize program costs; and

children.label: vii
children.text: other matters, including the manner in which such proponents propose to fund the program to ensure its cost effectiveness, as well as any relevant factors demonstrating the potential for, or necessary to obtain, the support and general cooperation of a substantial number of affected harvesters in the fishery (or portion of the fishery) for which the program is intended; and

children.label: B
children.text: proposes procedures for program participation (such as submission of owner bids under an auction system or fair market-value assessment), including any terms and conditions for participation, that the harvester proponents deem to be reasonably necessary to meet the program’s proposed objectives.

children.heading: Participation contracts
children.label: 4 Participation contracts
children.text: The Secretary shall contract with each person participating in a program, and each such contract shall, in addition to including such other matters as the Secretary deems necessary and appropriate to effectively implement each program (including penalties for contract non-performance) be consistent with the framework and implementing regulations and all other applicable law.

children.heading: Reduction auctions
children.label: 5 Reduction auctions
children.text: Each program not involving fair market assessment shall involve a reduction auction that scores the reduction price of each bid offer by the data relevant to each bidder under an appropriate fisheries productivity factor. If the Secretary accepts bids, the Secretary shall accept responsive bids in the rank order of their bid scores, starting with the bid whose reduction price is the lowest percentage of the productivity factor, and successively accepting each additional responsive bid in rank order until either there are no more responsive bids or acceptance of the next bid would cause the total value of bids accepted to exceed the amount of funds available for the program.

children.heading: Bid invitations
children.label: 6 Bid invitations
children.text: Each program shall proceed by the Secretary issuing invitations to bid setting out the terms and conditions for participation consistent with the framework and implementing regulations. Each bid that the Secretary receives in response to the invitation to bid shall constitute an irrevocable offer from the bidder.
